evals.prompt: Use authsome to log into GitHub. Once connected, use the credentials to run a curl request to fetch my GitHub user profile.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs authsome list, initiates the login flow for github, verifies it is connected, then uses authsome run --provider github -- curl https://api.github.com/user to fetch the profile. Does not export or echo any tokens.

evals.prompt: I need to run my OpenAI-powered script at ./scripts/summarise.py. Set up auth if needed and run it.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs authsome list to check if openai is connected. If not connected, runs authsome login openai. Then runs authsome run --provider openai -- python ./scripts/summarise.py. Does not export or echo any credentials.

evals.prompt: Register a custom provider named 'acme-internal' for our internal company tool that uses an API key with the header X-API-Key. Then log in to it.
evals.expected_output: The agent fetches the provider registration guide from the raw GitHub URL, writes the API key provider JSON to /tmp/acme-internal.json, runs authsome register /tmp/acme-internal.json, then runs authsome login acme-internal. Does not place the file directly in ~/.authsome/providers/.

evals.prompt: Can you list my last 5 starred repositories on GitHub?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognises it needs GitHub auth without being explicitly told to use authsome, runs authsome list to check connection state, logs in if needed, then runs authsome run --provider github -- curl 'https://api.github.com/user/starred?per_page=5' to fetch the results.
